item.title: Listen to Madonna’s New Song “I Feel So Free”
item.description: Fresh off her surprise appearance at Sabrina Carpenter’s Coachella set, the first preview of Confessions II
item.dc:creator: Hattie Lindert

item.title: Life for Rent
item.description: Each Sunday, Pitchfork takes an in-depth look at a significant album from the past, and any record not in our archives is eligible. Today, we revisit the UK singer’s second album, a fascinatingly normal early-aughts pop record from a woman whose still waters run deep.
item.dc:creator: Shaad D’Souza

item.title: “Dum Maro Dum”
item.description: Every Saturday, we’re going deep on one song we’ve never reviewed before. This week, we look back at one of the biggest tracks in Bollywood history. Discussed: unconventional characters, Western psychedelia, weed smoking, and the playback singer as actress.
item.dc:creator: Vrinda Jagota

item.title: Nine Inch Noize
item.description: Trent Reznor reconfigures his band and his music for a sort of live remix album that is unfussy, dancey, and, if you can believe it, fun.
item.dc:creator: Will Lynch

item.title: “High Rollers”
item.description: The Canadian producer’s acid-tinged new beat banger is an addictive highlight from his first new album in 10 years.
item.dc:creator: Anna Gaca

item.title: “Tape 05”
item.description: The psychedelic Scottish electronic duo returns from the depths with a cryptic yet beautiful transmission.
item.dc:creator: Alex Suskind

item.title: Wu Lyf Postpone North American Shows
item.description: In a statement, the band cited “delays in visa processing” and wrote that they’ll “be announcing new dates as soon as possible”
item.dc:creator: Walden Green

item.title: Listen to Kacey Musgraves’ New Song “Middle of Nowhere”
item.description: The title track of her new album precedes her late-notice set at Coachella this weekend
item.dc:creator: Jazz Monroe

item.title: 12 New Albums You Should Listen to Now: Tokischa, Nine Inch Noize, and More
item.description: Stream new releases from Tokischa, Nine Inch Noize and Jessie Ware
item.dc:creator: Hattie Lindert, Kiana Mickles, Nina Corcoran, Jazz Monroe

item.title: Tyla Taps Zara Larsson for New Song “She Did It Again”
item.description: It's their first collaboration, and the latest preview of Tyla’s second album, A-Pop
item.dc:creator: Hattie Lindert

item.title: Slayyyter Drops New Song “Broke Bitch Free$tyle”
item.description: The live staple gets an official release ahead of her Coachella Weekend 2 set
item.dc:creator: Walden Green

item.title: Olivia Rodrigo Gets Versailles to Herself in New “Drop Dead” Video
item.description: The lead single from You Seem Pretty Sad for a Girl So in Love is out now
item.dc:creator: Hattie Lindert

item.title: M.I.7
item.description: Buckle up for M.I.A.’s surprisingly moving Christian album, a bass-driven, gospel-infused testament to personal salvation.
item.dc:creator: Chal Ravens

item.title: KONNAKOL
item.description: The English singer reunites with an old collaborator and digs deeper into his South Asian musical inheritance, but his fifth album of R&B-laced pop feels only slightly more realized.
item.dc:creator: Sameer Rao

item.title: Sanctions
item.description: The pioneering alt-country band returns with its first album in 30 years—a set of cryptic, languid dirges that feels defiantly out-of-time.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld

item.title: Bloodzebra
item.description: On a collaborative record where slurred Mandarin intersects with stuttering synths, the Chengdu rapper and his mentor interrogate the disconnect between social life and our internal worlds.
item.dc:creator: James Gui

item.title: D4vd Arrested for Murder of Celeste Rivas
item.description: The 14-year-old girl’s body was found in the singer-songwriter’s Tesla last September
item.dc:creator: Hattie Lindert

item.title: Massive Attack’s First Song in Six Years Features Tom Waits
item.description: “Seen within the American emergency, this track contains pulses of callous impulse and abandoned mind,” the duo says of “Boots on the Ground”
item.dc:creator: Kiana Mickles

item.title: Neko Case’s Thelma & Louise Musical to Debut This Fall
item.description: The movie’s original writer penned the book while Case wrote the music and lyrics
item.dc:creator: Nina Corcoran

item.title: “trampstamp”
item.description: The Nashville-based songwriter is driving mules across the astral plane on a folksy and hypnotic new single.
item.dc:creator: Lily Goldberg

item.title: “Boots on the Ground”
item.description: The UK group returns after six years and casts one of America’s great troubadours in a magnetic, harrowing role.
item.dc:creator: Philip Sherburne

item.title: Coachella 2026 Weekend 2 YouTube Live Stream Schedule & Details
item.description: Missed the festival sets last weekend? Now you can see Sabrina Carpenter, Justin Bieber, Karol G, and more perform live
item.dc:creator: Nina Corcoran

item.title: Charli XCX Is Making a Rock Album
item.description: “I think the dance floor is dead,” she told Pitchfork contributor Laura Snapes in a new British Vogue cover story
item.dc:creator: Walden Green

item.title: “Mob Untied”
item.description: A shooting in Houston last week turned the Detroit rapper into a cult anti-hero overnight. Does tabloid rap beef on social media have a limit?
item.dc:creator: Alphonse Pierre

item.title: Listen to Lana Del Rey’s Theme for the New James Bond… Video Game
item.description: “First Light” was written by the singer and Bond composer David Arnold
item.dc:creator: Alex Suskind

item.title: “flee”
item.description: Despair and anger meet fluttering snares and sentimental strings on the duo’s dreamy but intense new track.
item.dc:creator: Olivier Lafontant

item.title: Boards of Canada Return With First New Music in 13 Years
item.description: After a series of cryptic posters and creepy VHS tapes, the duo has released a mysterious piece of music
item.dc:creator: Jazz Monroe

item.title: Lyra Pramuk Announces Hymnal (Resung) and Shares New Djrum Rework
item.description: The new spin on her 2025 album also features Verraco, Laurel Halo, and more
item.dc:creator: Jazz Monroe

item.title: AMOR & DROGA
item.description: Stretching herself across dembow, electro pop, and even surf rock, the Dominican firebrand vies for stardom, keeps it weird, and shows how far she’s come.
item.dc:creator: E.R. Pulgar

item.title: EMOTIONS
item.description: On his lethargic new album, the controversy-courting Georgia rapper has never sounded less interesting, engaged, or inspired.
item.dc:creator: Olivier Lafontant
